research_grade_baseline.device: Pupil Labs Core (open-source 200 Hz binocular headset)
research_grade_baseline.spatial_accuracy_deg: 0.6 (best-case 2D mapping, sub-1 deg typical); validation studies report ~1.5 deg mean angular offset
research_grade_baseline.spatial_source: pupil-labs.com/products/core/tech-specs; Ehinger et al. 2019 PMC6625505 vs EyeLink 1000
research_grade_baseline.price_usd_estimate: Pupil Core typically ~$2,000-$3,000 USD per headset (DIY frame option lowers cost). Pupil Neon turnkey = $7,499. The $500 figure cited in N-21 is likely a refurb/DIY frame + camera estimate, not a new MSRP.

boly_min_1.5deg_required.WebGazer_margin: fails by 2.5 deg (4.0 deg actual vs 1.5 deg required)

okn_specific_evidence.consumer_webcam_okn_detection_accuracy: 89-93% sensitivity, 98% specificity (Turuwhenua et al. PMC4848063 / MDPI healthcare 10(7):1281 2022)
okn_specific_evidence.vs_boly_2017_eye_only_decoder: Boly OKN-only SVM = 88% (research-grade tracker); webcam OKN classifiers reach 89-93% on global-motion stimuli with low-cost trackers
okn_specific_evidence.interpretation: Webcam-grade OKN direction classification is FEASIBLE for the bistable plaid task IF sample rate >= 30 Hz and accuracy <= 1 deg are achieved — i.e. GazeRecorder-class hardware, NOT WebGazer-class.
